seoTitle: Connect Amazon Business to QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Automate itemized purchase tracking by connecting your Amazon Business account to QuickBooks Online for cleaner expense records.
intro: Guessing what was in a mixed Amazon shipment makes bookkeeping harder than it needs to be. This guide shows how to connect your Amazon Business account to QuickBooks Online so itemized purchase data imports automatically.

steps.heading: Find the connector app
steps.body: Go to Transactions or Apps in the sidebar and search for Amazon Business Purchases.

steps.heading: Start the connection
steps.body: Click Get App Now to begin the automated security connection process.

steps.heading: Authorize your account
steps.body: Log in with your Amazon Business credentials on the secure redirected authorization portal.

steps.heading: Confirm sync permissions
steps.body: Review the data sync parameters and confirm read-write permissions to establish the API bridge.

steps.heading: Set your import window and map categories
steps.body: Choose a historical import timeframe, then match specific Amazon items to accurate Chart of Accounts expense lines.

faq.question: Does the connector break down mixed shipments into individual items?
faq.answer: Yes, the integration pulls full invoices with individual line items rather than a single lump charge.

faq.question: Can I map different Amazon items to different expense categories?
faq.answer: Yes, the category mapping step lets you match specific items to accurate Chart of Accounts expense lines.

faq.question: How do I verify a purchase against its original invoice?
faq.answer: Check the attached document area, which shows the original Amazon invoice PDF for reference.

faq.question: Will this replace my credit card bank feed for Amazon charges?
faq.answer: You'll likely still want to use the bank transactions tab to match the automated Amazon records against your actual card or bank deposits.
